title: exotic peppery tunisian vegetarian tagine
description: A flavorful and aromatic North African stew originating from Tunisia, characterized by its bold and peppery flavors, made with a variety of colorful vegetables and fragrant spices.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a ttukbaegi or heavy clay pot over medium heat.
Add the onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to soften, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, carrots, red bell peppers, and eggplant; cook, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes or until the vegetables are tender.
Add the cumin, coriander, paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper; stir to combine.
Add the white wine vinegar and cook for 1 minute, scraping the bottom of the pot with a wooden spoon.
Stir in the chopped parsley and cook for 1 minute more.
Transfer the tagine to a serving platter and serve hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Serve the tagine with couscous or crusty bread, if desired.
Garnish with lemon wedges or additional parsley, if desired.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 5 days or in the freezer for up to 3 months.
Reheat the tagine in the microwave or oven until hot and steaming, adding a splash of water if necessary to prevent the tagine from drying out.
Freeze the tagine for up to 3 months and reheat it in the microwave or oven until hot and steaming, adding a splash of water if necessary to prevent the tagine from drying out.
